subject: Re: Crash on subtomogram export
body:
Hi James,

The problem most likely lies with a specific tilt series (or particle) and
as a quick check you could remove the troublesome tomogram's particles from
the star file and try again. I've had a similar issue where my particle
coordinates were outside the tomogram (i.e. the star file contained
negative numbers). You can look in the subtomo folder at the particles
being extracted and see if it stops mid-way during a tomogram extraction
(the next particle will be your problem) and check your star file for
anything odd related to that particle.

Cheers,

Mat

On Tue, 1 Aug 2023 at 15:22, James Hooker <jhooker@mrc-lmb.cam.ac.uk> wrote:

> Hello all,
>
> I'm trying to export some subtomograms, but after Warp completes the boxes
> for 1-and-a-bit tilt series, it announces that it has crashed. One of its
> threads keeps writing out subtomograms for quite a while, but not all of
> them, and no star file gets written. Is this something anyone has seen
> before?
>
> Best,
> James
>
> --
